USDA launches TRUMP mission to Vietnam following record 45% surge in agricultural exports. - FreshFruitPortal.com # USDA launches TRUMP mission to Vietnam to expand market access for American farmers. The US Department of Agriculture (USDA) launched a new Trade Reciprocity for US Manufacturers and Producers (TRUMP) Mission to Vietnam. The initiative aims to **open new markets, strengthen export opportunities, and secure fair, reciprocal trade** for American farmers, ranchers, and producers. “This mission will build on that momentum by expanding market access, connecting suppliers with new buyers, and advancing negotiations under President Trump’s strategy to deliver fair, enforceable trade for American farmers and ranchers,” read the statement. Later this year, the delegation will return to Vietnam with a broader agribusiness trade mission to continue expanding opportunities for US food and agricultural exports. **USDA Trade Mission arrives in Taiwan to boost American ag exports**. MDARD launches program to connect communities to farmers - Farm Progress Michigan Bits: Scio Township preserves more land; self-paced pesticide applicator fruit pest management course offered; free virtual gardening class helps beginners. REGIONAL FOOD SYSTEMS: A new Farmers to Markets program is designed to help boost farmers’ sales at local markets and increase community access to Michigan-grown foods. The Michigan Department of Agriculture and Rural Development and the Michigan Farmers Market Association announced a new Farmers to Markets program to help boost farmers’ sales at local markets and increase community access to Michigan-grown foods. “The Farmers to Markets program will strengthen regional food systems by supporting reliable season-long sales for Michigan farmers market vendors, reducing risks associated with new market entry while promoting sales of local agriculture products,” MDARD Director Tim Boring said. Michigan State University’s Pesticide Safety Education Program has released a new self-paced online course designed to help pesticide applicators strengthen their fruit pest management skills and earn restricted-use pesticide recertification credits. https://www.farmprogress.com/farm-business/mdard-launches-program-to-connect-communities-to-farmers
